title: Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam review – an electric debut set on Manchester’s Curry Mile
description:
Written in breathless multilingual prose, this coming-of-age meets state-of-the-nation novel is an incredible literary performance

Three twentysomethings “drive and dream of an impossible night on an endless street. moving as a massive through mad sticky traffic, destination: where else? manchester, wilmslow road, the curry mile, yo!” Thus opens Sufiyaan Salam’s high-octane debut novel, written largely in gen Z lowercase – and you’re in for a ride.

The Boyz are British Pakistani f
hook: Three Manchester twentysomethings speaking in breathless multilingual prose—a debut that treats the Curry Mile like it's the whole world.

title: The Cartoonist Who Mocked the Madness of Modernism
description: "With biting satire, Alan Dunn captured how 20th-century architectural trends left everyday Americans astonished, baffled, and enraged."
hook: Alan Dunn's cartoons mocked modernist architecture by showing how it actually made Americans feel: baffled, enraged, astonished.

title: Life With Locked-in Syndrome: ‘Despite Everything, You Are Alive’
description: "Matt Rudd has remarkable conversations with three Britons who, after life-changing accidents, have fully active minds but cannot move or speak, and can communicate only via the blink of an eye."
hook: Three people trapped inside their own bodies share what it means to think, feel, and love when you can only blink to speak.

title: The Day YouTube Wept: Marcus Batto’s Found-Footage Memorial to Michael Jackson
description: A day in the life of the internet is impossible to reconstruct as a feature film. Th
hook: Found footage of strangers mourning Michael Jackson becomes an accidental monument to how we grieve celebrities we've never met.

title: Back Cove
description: The cordgrass blurs to gray suggestion.Eiders merge—dark smudges on darker water.The cove’s edge wavers, uncertain. I know the herring gull is thereby sound: the measured drop and crack,shell against rock, against rock again.Twenty feet away, maybe thirty— The gull works. I hear the lift, rise, release.The clatter of failure. Then nothing.I mistake industry for talent. […]
hook: A poem where a gull cracks shells and you hear it before you see it—sensory precision that makes attention feel like discovery.
